text.title: AI Product Marketing Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Product Marketing Patterns application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Public demos disclose variance: 'results vary; try multiple prompts'. Top-of-demo includes 'best of 3 runs' OR average-performance note.
rule.rationale: Hiding variance trains buyers to expect deterministic output, fueling churn; disclosure cuts churn 40-60%.

rule.statement: Marketing copy leads with the workflow ('draft 30 cold emails in 5 min'), not the feature ('GPT-4 powered email writer').
rule.rationale: Workflow value persists across model versions; feature framing locks marketing to model lifecycles.

rule.statement: Avoid public 'AI vs Competitor X' pages. LLM lottery means any single run can favor the competitor and screenshot-spread. Use 'AI for [workflow]' pages instead.
rule.rationale: Comparison pages assume deterministic output. AI products fail this assumption catastrophically.

rule.statement: Stack trust signals 2× normal SaaS density: founder-bio + named customers + case-study videos + SOC2 logos + data-handling page.
rule.rationale: Trust deficit on AI is the #1 conversion blocker; 2× density lifts AI conversion 20-50% (CXL/Wynter tests).

rule.statement: Ship a public prompt / use-case library. Each entry has expected output + caveats. Library = SEO + onboarding + trust + workflow demo.
rule.rationale: AI users undervalue products they cannot productively prompt.
